details.body: In `try_reconcile_success` (daemon_loop.rs ~1480-1500), when `project_store.read_project_record` or `run_snapshot_read.read_run_snapshot` fails for a task that DOES have a `task_source` (i.e. is milestone-linked), the function returns early after an eprintln but does NOT transition the milestone to Failed/needs-operator. This leaves the bead open with no operator signal. The early returns on data-read failures should attempt the same `update_status → Failed` transition that the `BrCloseFailed` path performs, so the controller knows intervention is needed.

Add at least one test that exercises the full `reconcile_success` function end-to-end, including the `update_milestone_state` step (step 3). Currently all 8 tests cover only the sub-functions (`close_bead_idempotent`, `sync_after_close`, `capture_next_step_hint`). The milestone recording path through `FsMilestoneSnapshotStore` / `FsMilestoneJournalStore` / `FsTaskRunLineageStore` is completely untested, leaving the integration between bead closure and milestone progress update unverified.

src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1441-1442 invokes success reconciliation only after the task has fully completed, but src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1504-1532 then requires run_snapshot.active_run or interrupted_run to recover run_id/started_at. Successful runs clear both cursors before control returns to the daemon (src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:5530-5535), so the normal happy path falls into the None branch and returns before any bead close, sync, or milestone update happens.

In multi-repo mode, process_task_multi_repo passes store_dir into try_reconcile_success (src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:603-606, 911-913), but the task's Ralph project is created/read under repo_root (src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:709-710). try_reconcile_success uses its base_dir argument for read_project_record/read_run_snapshot (src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1485-1507), so reconciliation looks in the daemon data directory instead of the checked-out repo and returns early without touching the bead.

src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:141-144 only runs br sync --flush-only when close_bead_idempotent reports a fresh close. That breaks the required crash-safe idempotency: if the process dies after br close mutates local bead state but before sync, a rerun will observe the bead as already closed and permanently skip the flush. A fresh BrMutationAdapter starts with no pending-mutation knowledge (src/adapters/br_process.rs:449-463), so the already-closed path still needs to issue the post-close sync.

update_milestone_state receives task_id but drops it when persisting the success outcome: it only calls record_bead_completion with bead/project/run metadata (src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:238-271). The durable lineage row and completion journal payload have no task_id field (src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:354-376, 475-500), so this change still does not record the required task_id + bead_id + outcome + timestamp linkage outcome.

details.body: In daemon_loop.rs, both call sites invoke `mark_completed` BEFORE `try_reconcile_success` (lines 911-914 and 1441-1444). If the daemon crashes in this window, the task is permanently marked completed and will never be reprocessed, so reconciliation never runs: the bead stays open in br, sync never happens, the milestone is not updated, and no operator notification occurs. Either (a) reconciliation should run before mark_completed, or (b) a startup recovery sweep should detect completed tasks whose beads are still open and retry reconciliation, or (c) the reconciliation outcome should be persisted as a flag that a separate recovery path checks.

In service.rs, `merge_completion_journal_details` (lines 1048-1078) does not merge the newly added `task_id` field from `CompletionJournalDetails`. If `task_id` is ever populated (the schema field exists at model.rs:493), the merge function will silently drop it during journal event deduplication. Add `if merged.task_id.is_none() { merged.task_id = requested.task_id.clone(); }` to match the pattern used for `run_id`, `plan_hash`, and `outcome_detail`.

`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:913-914` calls `try_reconcile_success(store_dir, repo_root, ...)`, but inside `try_reconcile_success` the code uses `base_dir` for milestone mutations (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1523-1529`, `1590-1602`) and `reconcile_success` passes that same path into `record_bead_completion` (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:147-158`, `262-276`). In multi-repo mode milestone bundles are materialized under `workspace_dir`/`repo_root`, not the daemon shard (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1217-1224`, `2033-2040`), and the initial bead-start sync also uses the workspace base dir (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:551-582`). As written, success reconciliation updates the wrong filesystem tree in multi-repo mode, so milestone progress/journal writes and `needs_operator` transitions will miss the real milestone files.

`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1586-1588` constructs `BrMutationAdapter::new()`, `BrAdapter::new()`, and `BvAdapter::new()` without setting a working directory. Those adapters default `working_dir` to `None` (`src/adapters/br_process.rs:355-360`, `src/adapters/bv_process.rs:369-374`), so `br show/close/sync` and `bv --robot-next` run in the daemon process's current directory instead of the repository that owns the target `.beads/` graph. In multi-repo mode that can close or sync the wrong repo, or fail depending on the daemon launch cwd. These repo-scoped commands need to be anchored to `repo_root` via `with_working_dir(...)`.

The new `task_id` fields are never actually written to the durable completion record. `update_milestone_state` only stuffs the daemon task id into a free-form `outcome_detail` string (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:258-273`). Then `TaskRunEntry::completion_journal_details()` ignores `self.task_id` (`src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:428-436`), `render_completion_journal_details` hardcodes `task_id: None` (`src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:496-512`), and the lineage finalization path only updates `outcome_detail`/timestamps without populating `TaskRunEntry.task_id` (`src/adapters/fs.rs:3867-3890`). The result is that the milestone completion journal still does not record `task_id`, despite the new schema fields, and the linkage is only recoverable by parsing a string.

details.body: In `src/adapters/fs.rs`, `CompletionJournalDetails::render()` (line 2857) passes `None` for the `task_id` parameter instead of `self.task_id.as_deref()`. This means any completion journal event that goes through the merge or repair code paths (lines 2951, 2996, 3017) will silently lose its `task_id` linkage. The fix is to change `None` to `self.task_id.as_deref()` on line 2857.

In `src/adapters/fs.rs`, `CompletionJournalDetails::merge()` (lines 2816-2846) does not merge the `task_id` field. It should add a block like `if merged.task_id.is_none() { merged.task_id = requested.task_id.clone(); }` after the existing `outcome_detail` merge, and should also check for conflicts on `task_id` alongside the other optional fields.

`src/adapters/fs.rs:2816-2858` and `src/adapters/fs.rs:2948-2996` still handle `CompletionJournalDetails` as if it had no `task_id`: the fs-side merge logic does not conflict-check or merge `task_id`, and `render()` always serializes `task_id` as `None`, even though the canonical model now includes it in `src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:492-514`. Update the fs journal dedupe/repair paths to preserve and serialize `task_id` so idempotent reruns or stale-event repairs do not silently strip the structured task-to-bead linkage from milestone completion events.

details.message: backend invocation failed for contract 'plan_and_implement' via 'claude': Claude returned empty result with no structured_output (contract: plan_and_implement, stdout_len: 932, stderr_len: 0, session_policy: ReuseIfAllowed, stdout_tail: "vice_tier\":\"standard\",\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":145972,\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0},\"inference_geo\":\"\",\"iterations\":[],\"speed\":\"standard\"},\"modelUsage\":{\"claude-opus-4-6\":{\"inputTokens\":15,\"outputTokens\":3802,\"cacheReadInputTokens\":2000863,\"cacheCreationInputTokens\":145972,\"webSearchRequests\":0,\"costUSD\":2.0078815,\"contextWindow\":200000,\"maxOutputTokens\":64000}},\"permission_denials\":[],\"terminal_reason\":\"completed\",\"fast_mode_state\":\"off\",\"uuid\":\"47be93f1-01fa-46e7-baf5-2051c7ed3357\"}\n", stderr_tail: "")

details.body: In daemon_loop.rs line 1635-1637, `ReconciliationError::MilestoneUpdateFailed` is handled by only logging to stderr, without calling `mark_milestone_failed`. This means a milestone update failure leaves the milestone in whatever state it was in (potentially Active/InProgress), with no operator signal. The bead is already closed and synced in br at this point, so the br state and milestone state are now inconsistent. Consider calling `mark_milestone_failed` for this error variant as well, or at minimum documenting why this asymmetry is intentional.

The task_id extraction logic (`detail.strip_prefix("task_id=")`) is duplicated in three places in fs.rs (lines 3420, 3494, 3919). Extract this into a shared helper function (e.g., `fn extract_task_id_from_detail(detail: &str) -> Option<&str>`) to reduce duplication and ensure the parsing convention stays consistent if it needs to evolve.

In src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1635-1636, ReconciliationError::MilestoneUpdateFailed is only logged, but both success call sites immediately mark the daemon task completed afterward (src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:913-915 and src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1444-1446). Because reconcile_success closes/syncs the bead before update_milestone_state runs (src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:138-158 and src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:237-280), any snapshot/journal/lineage write failure leaves the bead closed while milestone progress, journal, and linkage stay stale, with no Failed/needs-operator transition and no retry path. MilestoneUpdateFailed should trigger the same operator-visible failure handling as BR failures, or the task should remain non-terminal so reconciliation can be retried.

The new reconciliation path still silently drops pre-reconciliation metadata failures. In src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1489-1505, an invalid project_id or unreadable project record returns from try_reconcile_success before any operator signal is raised, and the callers then mark the task completed anyway (src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:913-915 and src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1444-1446). For milestone-backed tasks, that skips bead close, sync, and milestone updates entirely with no retry path. This path should not fall through to mark_completed; it needs to keep the task non-terminal or fail/quarantine it so an operator can repair the missing project metadata.

details.message: backend invocation failed for contract 'plan_and_implement' via 'claude': invalid Claude result JSON: expected value at line 1 column 1 (contract: plan_and_implement, result_len: 94, stdout_len: 1049, result_tail: "All 4 amendments implemented, all 757 tests passing, formatting clean, no new clippy warnings.", stderr_tail: "")

details.body: In `try_reconcile_success` (daemon_loop.rs:1678-1688), when `BrCloseFailed` or `BrSyncFailed` occurs, `mark_milestone_failed` is called but the function still returns `true`, causing the task to be marked `completed`. Per the spec 'If br close fails, leave the bead open and transition the controller to a needs_operator state', the milestone transitions correctly, but the bead remains permanently un-closeable because no retry mechanism exists once the task is completed. Consider returning `false` (marking the task failed) for `BrCloseFailed`/`BrSyncFailed` so the task can be retried, or document that operator intervention is the only path to close the orphaned bead.

`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1678-1688` converts `BrCloseFailed` and `BrSyncFailed` into a milestone failure but still returns `true`, and both success paths immediately mark the daemon task completed afterward (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:943-959` and `src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1491-1494`). Retries are only allowed for failed or aborted tasks (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/task_service.rs:453-459`), so a `br close`/`br sync` failure can leave the bead open or unsynced with no automated recovery path while the task is reported as completed. These reconciliation errors need to keep the task non-completed, or otherwise enqueue a real retry, instead of being swallowed.

`src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:160-172` captures the `bv --robot-next` result in `ReconciliationOutcome`, but `try_reconcile_success` collapses that outcome to a `bool` (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1529`) and the only handling of `outcome.next_step_hint` is a `println!` (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1671-1676`). Nothing persists the hint or returns it to later selection logic, so the controller has no durable next-step hint to consult after reconciliation finishes.

details.body: In `try_reconcile_success` (daemon_loop.rs ~line 1599-1643), when journal reading fails or run_id extraction fails, the function calls `mark_milestone_failed` then returns `Ok(())`. This means the bead is never closed (reconcile_success is never called), but the task proceeds to `mark_completed`. The bead remains permanently open with no retry path since the task is 'completed'. These metadata-read failures should return `Err(...)` instead of `Ok(())` so the task is marked failed and can be retried on a subsequent daemon cycle, consistent with the spec requirement that bead-open failures transition to needs_operator state without silently swallowing the error.

In `try_reconcile_success` (daemon_loop.rs ~line 1695-1697), when `reconcile_success` returns `ReconciliationError::MilestoneUpdateFailed`, the code calls `mark_milestone_failed` and returns `Ok(())`. At this point, br close and sync have already succeeded (steps 1-2 completed before step 3), so the bead is closed but milestone progress is not tracked. This is arguably acceptable since the bead IS closed, but the task-to-bead linkage and journal entry are lost. Consider whether this should also return `Err(...)` to mark the task failed and force a retry, since `record_bead_completion` is idempotent and would succeed on retry if the underlying FS issue is transient.

`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1599-1643` and `src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1695-1697` convert several reconciliation failures into `Ok(())` after a best-effort milestone failure update. Both callers treat `Ok(())` as success and immediately mark the daemon task completed (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:914-942` and `src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1471-1488`). If the project journal is unreadable or missing run metadata, the bead is never closed or synced; if `record_bead_completion` fails, the milestone progress/journal/linkage write is missing. In both cases the task becomes terminal `completed`, so there is no retry path even though reconciliation did not actually succeed. These branches should return an error (or otherwise keep the task retryable) instead of falling through as success.

`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1575-1592` ignores the result of `milestone_service::update_status(... MilestoneStatus::Failed ...)` with `let _ =`, including the `BrCloseFailed`/`BrSyncFailed` path at `src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1688-1693`. If the status update itself fails (snapshot corruption, I/O failure, invalid transition), the controller never actually reaches the required needs-operator state even though the reconciliation error is surfaced elsewhere. The failed-status write needs to be checked and propagated/logged as its own hard failure instead of being silently discarded.

details.message: backend invocation failed for contract 'plan_and_implement' via 'claude': Claude returned empty result with no structured_output (contract: plan_and_implement, stdout_len: 951, stderr_len: 0, session_policy: ReuseIfAllowed, stdout_tail: "\":\"standard\",\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":145875,\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0},\"inference_geo\":\"\",\"iterations\":[],\"speed\":\"standard\"},\"modelUsage\":{\"claude-opus-4-6\":{\"inputTokens\":16,\"outputTokens\":5069,\"cacheReadInputTokens\":2148085,\"cacheCreationInputTokens\":145875,\"webSearchRequests\":0,\"costUSD\":2.1125662499999995,\"contextWindow\":200000,\"maxOutputTokens\":64000}},\"permission_denials\":[],\"terminal_reason\":\"completed\",\"fast_mode_state\":\"off\",\"uuid\":\"9ae4f6eb-b336-47e2-9670-6fc7d454c9ef\"}\n", stderr_tail: "")

details.body: In try_reconcile_success (daemon_loop.rs ~1703), the MilestoneUpdateFailed arm now calls mark_milestone_failed which does update_status(…, Failed). But this occurs AFTER br close + sync already succeeded (steps 1-2 in reconcile_success). The bead is now permanently closed yet the task is marked failed with no completion record in the milestone. If the operator retries, the bead will be detected as already-closed (idempotent path), but record_bead_completion will attempt to write a duplicate completion event. Add a comment documenting this partial-commit state is intentional and safe on retry, OR restructure so that milestone-update failures after a successful br close are logged as warnings but still allow the task to proceed to mark_completed (since the critical bead mutation did succeed).

In the single-repo process_task path (daemon_loop.rs ~1474), mark_failed is called with the ? operator, so if mark_failed itself fails (e.g., disk full, corrupt store), the error propagates and skips try_push_failed_task_branch and release_task_lease. The multi-repo path has the same pattern (line 922) but also skips label sync. Consider catching the mark_failed error so the lease is always released, preventing a stuck lease on store write failure.

`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1600-1676` reconstructs only `run_id` and `started_at`, then uses `Utc::now()` as the completion time at `src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1654` and passes that through to `record_bead_completion` in `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:147-158` and `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:267-280`. In both completion paths, reconciliation can run after extra PR/cleanup work (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:840-914` and `src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1444-1472`), so the milestone journal/linkage timestamp can drift arbitrarily from the actual durable `run_completed` event. The existing milestone terminal-sync path already treats the terminal journal event timestamp as authoritative (`src/cli/run.rs:317-345`); this handler should do the same or it will record late/misordered completion events and inaccurate success timestamps.

`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1575-1597` still makes the required needs-operator transition best-effort only: an invalid milestone ID is silently skipped by `if let Ok(milestone_id) = ...`, and an `update_status(..., Failed, ...)` failure is only logged. The caller then propagates only the original reconciliation error (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1696-1708`), so the daemon task becomes failed while the milestone can remain `running`/`paused` instead of entering the controller’s failed/needs-operator state. This failure to transition the milestone should be surfaced as its own hard error instead of being reduced to a log line.

details.message: backend invocation failed for contract 'plan_and_implement' via 'claude': Claude returned empty result with no structured_output (contract: plan_and_implement, stdout_len: 954, stderr_len: 0, session_policy: ReuseIfAllowed, stdout_tail: "standard\",\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":76276,\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0},\"inference_geo\":\"\",\"iterations\":[],\"speed\":\"standard\"},\"modelUsage\":{\"claude-opus-4-6\":{\"inputTokens\":16568,\"outputTokens\":27780,\"cacheReadInputTokens\":2025804,\"cacheCreationInputTokens\":234728,\"webSearchRequests\":0,\"costUSD\":3.2572919999999996,\"contextWindow\":200000,\"maxOutputTokens\":64000}},\"permission_denials\":[],\"terminal_reason\":\"completed\",\"fast_mode_state\":\"off\",\"uuid\":\"8d6ebd33-aa4b-453d-baf8-5271b86dbb4d\"}\n", stderr_tail: "")

details.body: In daemon_loop.rs at line 939, when `mark_failed` itself fails, the code falls through to `try_push_failed_task_branch` and `release_task_lease` (lines 947-951), leaving the task in an `InProgress` state in the store with no lease. On the next daemon cycle, the task will be picked up again. If the underlying `mark_failed` failure is persistent (e.g., corrupt store file), this creates an infinite retry loop with CRITICAL log spam every cycle. Consider adding a bounded retry counter or a fallback that writes a sentinel file to prevent reprocessing, or at minimum document the expected recovery path.

The MilestoneUpdateFailed change at daemon_loop.rs lines 1769-1783 downgrades milestone update failure to a warning and proceeds to mark_completed. This means if record_bead_completion fails (e.g., disk full, permissions), the milestone snapshot will permanently show the bead as in-progress (or with stale progress counts) while the task is marked completed and the bead is closed in br. There is no automated recovery path since mark_completed prevents reprocessing. Add a follow-up TODO or operator alert mechanism (beyond eprintln) to reconcile milestone state, or add a lightweight 'milestone repair' pass to the daemon's Phase 0 that detects closed beads with missing completion records.

Do not release the lease when `DaemonTaskService::mark_failed` itself returns an error in the reconciliation-failure path. The new handlers at [daemon_loop.rs#L916](/home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs#L916) and [daemon_loop.rs#L1486](/home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs#L1486) log the failure and still drop the lease, but `mark_failed` can fail before the failed state is durably written at [task_service.rs#L409](/home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/src/contexts/automation_runtime/task_service.rs#L409). Recovery only revisits `label_dirty` tasks in [daemon_loop.rs#L377](/home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs#L377), and normal scheduling only scans `Pending` tasks in [daemon_loop.rs#L548](/home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs#L548) and [daemon_loop.rs#L1029](/home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs#L1029), so a task can be stranded forever in `Claimed`/`Active` with no lease and no retry path.

details.body: In persist_next_step_hint (success_reconciliation.rs:316), std::fs::write is not atomic — a crash during write leaves a partial next_step_hint.json on disk. Downstream selection logic reading this file could deserialize a truncated JSON and either fail or mis-select. Use write-to-tempfile + rename pattern (atomic on POSIX) to prevent this.

persist_next_step_hint never deletes stale hint files. If a bead completes but bv returns None (no hint), the previous bead's hint file remains and could mislead downstream selection in the next cycle. Add cleanup: when next_step_hint is None, delete next_step_hint.json if it exists.

In `src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1787-1813`, do not swallow `ReconciliationError::MilestoneUpdateFailed`. `reconcile_success` closes and flushes the bead before it calls `update_milestone_state` (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:141-158`, `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:242-286`), but the daemon currently only logs that failure and then both success paths still mark the daemon task completed (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:966-967`, `src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1523-1525`). Since completed tasks are terminal and `retry_task` only accepts `Failed` or `Aborted` (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/task_service.rs:373-399`, `src/contexts/automation_runtime/task_service.rs:453-460`), any transient snapshot/journal/lineage write failure permanently loses the milestone progress update, completion journal entry, and durable task-to-bead linkage.

The replay path is still not fully crash-idempotent. `reconcile_success` always re-runs `br sync --flush-only` even when the bead is already closed (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:141-145`), and `try_reconcile_success` treats any replay-time `BrSyncFailed` as a hard reconciliation failure (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1774-1785`). Each replay constructs a fresh `BrMutationAdapter` with `has_unsync_mutations` reset to false (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1735-1740`, `src/adapters/br_process.rs:475-479`), so the code has no durable way to distinguish "close succeeded but flush never landed" from "close and flush already succeeded before the crash". A transient `br` outage after a fully successful first reconciliation can therefore incorrectly move an already-reconciled task into the failed/needs-operator path on replay.

details.message: backend invocation failed for contract 'plan_and_implement' via 'claude': invalid Claude result JSON: expected value at line 1 column 1 (contract: plan_and_implement, result_len: 86, stdout_len: 1025, result_tail: "All 4 round-10 amendments implemented, quality checks pass, ExecutionPayload produced.", stderr_tail: "")

details.body: In `success_reconciliation.rs:150-161`, the sync-failure tolerance when `was_already_closed=true` is unsound. The flag only proves the bead was closed before this invocation — not that sync ever completed. Scenario: first attempt closes the bead successfully but sync fails (returns `BrSyncFailed`), daemon marks the task Failed. On retry, `is_bead_already_closed` returns true (bead was closed locally), so `was_already_closed=true`. If sync fails again (same transient issue), the error is swallowed as non-fatal, milestone state is updated, and the task is marked Completed — but the close was never flushed to the remote store. Fix: use a more reliable signal for 'sync already completed' (e.g., check for a successful reconciliation journal entry from a prior run, or a durable marker file written after sync succeeds) rather than relying solely on bead-closed status.

Add a test for the `was_already_closed=true` + sync-failure path in `success_reconciliation.rs`. Currently there is no test exercising the new `Err(e) if was_already_closed` branch at line 152. A test should verify the behavior when sync fails on replay (bead already closed) vs. when sync fails on first close.

`src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:146-160` now suppresses `BrSyncFailed` whenever `was_already_closed` is true. That is not a safe proxy for "the earlier reconciliation fully succeeded": it is also the state you get after a crash between `br close` and `br sync --flush-only` (the comment at lines 141-145 already describes exactly that case). In that scenario, a replay that hits a transient `br sync` failure will now continue into milestone completion and task completion even though the close was never flushed. Please keep replay sync failures fatal unless you persist a separate durable marker proving the prior sync already landed.

`src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:176-189`, together with `capture_next_step_hint()` at `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:311-320` and `delete_stale_hint()` at `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:368-381`, now deletes `next_step_hint.json` whenever hint capture returns `None`. But `capture_next_step_hint()` returns `None` for every `bv` failure path as well as for a genuine "no hint" case, so a transient `bv` outage on a replay can erase a previously persisted hint even though no new authoritative result was obtained. Please distinguish "no recommendation" from execution failure, and only clear the file for the former.

details.body: In `persist_next_step_hint` (success_reconciliation.rs:335-371), the function assumes the milestone directory already exists when writing the hint file. If the directory doesn't exist (e.g., a race condition where milestone state was cleaned up), `std::fs::write` at line 346 will fail silently (logged at debug level). Consider adding a `create_dir_all` before the write, or document that the function's best-effort contract intentionally does not create directories.

`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1611-1628` and `src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1779-1814` move the milestone to `MilestoneStatus::Failed` for reconciliation errors, but `Failed` is terminal (`src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:80-103`). Every subsequent run start/resume goes back through `sync_milestone_bead_start` (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:1055-1057`, `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:1750-1756`), and `record_bead_start` rejects terminal milestones (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1896-1902`). In practice, a transient `br`/milestone-store outage now wedges the bead permanently: the daemon task can be retried, but the retry will fail before work restarts because the milestone can no longer accept a bead start. This needs a non-terminal needs-operator state, or an explicit reopen path before retrying.

details.message: backend invocation failed for contract 'plan_and_implement' via 'claude': invalid Claude result JSON: expected value at line 1 column 1 (contract: plan_and_implement, result_len: 822, stdout_len: 1782, result_tail: "Removed `mark_milestone_failed` closure and all 5 call sites from `try_reconcile_success`. The milestone now stays `Running` on reconciliation failure instead of being transitioned to `Failed` (terminal). This prevents the permanent wedge where `record_bead_start` rejects terminal milestones, blocking all retries. The task's `Err(...)` return is sufficient operator signal, and all reconciliation steps are idempotent for safe retry.\n\nAll checks pass: 757 tests, 0 clippy warnings in modified files, fmt clean.", stderr_tail: "")

details.body: Fix reconciliation failure recovery in `src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs`. The new logic assumes `retry_task` can re-run success reconciliation after `reconciliation_br_failed`, `reconciliation_metadata_error`, or `reconciliation_milestone_update_failed` (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1608-1616`, `src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1755-1762`), and it marks the daemon task failed/releases its lease on that path (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1496-1520`). But `retry_task` only moves the task back to `Pending` (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/task_service.rs:453-482`); the next dispatch rereads the project run snapshot (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:2373-2380`) and immediately rejects `RunStatus::Completed` before any reconciliation code runs (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:2958-3010`). After any transient reconciliation error, operators therefore get a permanent fail/retry/fail loop and the bead/milestone never gets reconciled. Add a dedicated reconciliation-only retry path, or reset/reopen the controller/run state so a retry can actually enter reconciliation.

details.body: Fix the reconciliation retry path in `src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs`. The new `RunStatus::Completed => Ok(())` branch at `src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:3007-3017` only bypasses dispatch; both task processors still rebase the worktree before dispatch (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:681-687` and `src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1408-1414`). After `try_reconcile_success` marks a task failed for reconciliation errors (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:913-964` and `src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1496-1521`), `retry_task` moves it back to `Pending` (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/task_service.rs:453-482`), so the retry still rebases the already-completed branch before it can re-enter reconciliation. If the default branch advanced or the preserved branch conflicts, the task fails again with a rebase error and the original transient `br`/milestone failure remains unrecoverable. Add a dedicated post-run retry path that skips rebase/run setup when the persisted run snapshot is already `Completed`, and add a regression test for retry-after-reconciliation-failure.

Fix resumed-run replays in the daemon reconciliation path. `try_reconcile_success` currently rewrites `started_at` to the latest `RunResumed` timestamp (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1685-1697`) and then passes that value straight into `record_bead_completion` (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:282-296`). But milestone lineage keeps the original bead-start timestamp when the same run resumes (`src/adapters/fs.rs:4054-4079`), and terminal replays reject any `started_at` mismatch (`src/adapters/fs.rs:3832-3855`). As a result, if a resumed run crashes or partially fails after the terminal lineage row has been written but before the daemon finishes reconciliation, every retry will hit an "already finalized" error instead of repairing the partial state. Use the durable bead-start timestamp from milestone state, or fall back to the same terminal-repair flow that `src/cli/run.rs:347-369` already uses for exact-attempt replays.

details.message: backend invocation failed for contract 'plan_and_implement' via 'claude': Claude returned empty result with no structured_output (contract: plan_and_implement, stdout_len: 933, stderr_len: 0, session_policy: ReuseIfAllowed, stdout_tail: "vice_tier\":\"standard\",\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":136904,\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0},\"inference_geo\":\"\",\"iterations\":[],\"speed\":\"standard\"},\"modelUsage\":{\"claude-opus-4-6\":{\"inputTokens\":51,\"outputTokens\":15926,\"cacheReadInputTokens\":6391328,\"cacheCreationInputTokens\":136904,\"webSearchRequests\":0,\"costUSD\":4.449719,\"contextWindow\":200000,\"maxOutputTokens\":64000}},\"permission_denials\":[],\"terminal_reason\":\"completed\",\"fast_mode_state\":\"off\",\"uuid\":\"92f9425e-eeca-400f-94c3-382fbe24918f\"}\n", stderr_tail: "")

details.body: Fix exact-attempt replay handling in the success-reconciliation path. `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:278-294` currently encodes the daemon `task_id` into `outcome_detail` (`"task_id=..."`) and then always calls `record_bead_completion`. That is not idempotent when the exact run was already finalized with any different success detail: terminal replays reject mismatched `outcome_detail` values in `src/adapters/fs.rs:3862-3896`, and journal repair rejects the same mismatch in `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1048-1083`. I reproduced this locally with an existing succeeded row whose detail was `"first bead completed"`; the replay fails with `task run ... is already finalized with outcome=succeeded`, leaving the bead already closed/synced but the daemon task permanently failing on every retry. Route exact-attempt replays through `repair_task_run_with_disposition` the way `src/cli/run.rs:347-369` already does, or add an explicit task-id parameter to the completion API so legacy success details can be preserved while backfilling linkage.

details.message: backend invocation failed for contract 'plan_and_implement' via 'claude': schema validation failed for stage 'plan_and_implement': missing field `change_summary`

details.body: Handle the successful `bv --robot-next` "no actionable items available" response in `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:167-181` and `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:361-370` (the current parser shape is `src/adapters/bv_process.rs:345-353`). Right now `capture_next_step_hint` only accepts `NextBeadResponse { id, title, ... }`; when `bv` succeeds with the message-only JSON it falls into `BvFailed`, and `reconcile_success` deliberately leaves any existing `next_step_hint.json` untouched. After the final actionable bead closes, that preserves a stale hint that can still point at an already-completed bead. Treat the message-only success payload as an explicit "no next hint" result and clear/remove any persisted hint in that case; add a regression test for `{"message":"No actionable items available"}`.

Fix the retry entry path for reconciliation-only failures. In src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:649-696 and src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1412-1446, the new `is_reconciliation_retry` guard runs only after `DaemonTaskService::claim_task(...)`. For the new reconciliation failure classes returned at src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1799-1828, `claim_task` still treats the retry as a generic failed retry (src/contexts/automation_runtime/task_service.rs:147-168) and `LeaseService::acquire` will create a worktree and attempt `try_resume_from_remote` before the reconciliation fast-path is reached (src/contexts/automation_runtime/lease_service.rs:210-263). As a result, a retry whose only remaining work is `br close/sync` plus milestone bookkeeping can still fail with unrelated worktree/network/auth errors before it ever reaches `try_reconcile_success`. The completed-run retry path needs to bypass or special-case worktree resume/acquire for the reconciliation-specific failure classes.

Persist and use the task’s actual workflow `run_id` instead of reconstructing it from the latest project journal entry. In src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1658-1779, `try_reconcile_success` finds the last `RunStarted`/`RunCompleted` in the project journal and assumes that run belongs to the failed daemon task. But `DaemonTask` does not durably store the workflow run identifier at all (src/contexts/automation_runtime/model.rs:49-87 only keeps `project_id` and `requirements_run_id`). If the project is rerun or manually resumed between the original reconciliation failure and a later `retry_task`, this code will reconcile against the newest run in the project journal rather than the run that actually belongs to the failed task, which can write the wrong completion timestamp / lineage repair or attach `task_id` to the wrong run. Store the workflow run_id on the task when dispatch starts/completes and read that here instead of guessing from 'latest RunStarted'.

details.body: In `success_reconciliation.rs:386-406`, `capture_next_step_hint` checks `e.raw_output()` for all error variants to detect a `BvMessageOnlyResponse`. This includes `BvExitError` (non-zero exit code), not just `BvParseError`. If bv crashes with a non-zero exit and its stdout happens to contain `{"message":"..."}`, the code would return `NoRecommendation` instead of `BvFailed`, causing `delete_stale_hint` to remove a previously valid hint file. The check should be restricted to `BvParseError` only (where bv exited 0 but returned unexpected JSON), not `BvExitError` (where bv genuinely failed). Fix: match on the specific error variant rather than using the generic `raw_output()` accessor — e.g., only extract raw output when `matches!(e, BvError::BvParseError { .. })`.

Gate the new Completed-run fast path to actual reconciliation-failure retries. Right now both task-processing paths classify any project snapshot with status Completed as a reconciliation retry (src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:686-694, src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1441-1449), and dispatch_in_worktree_with_service then short-circuits every Completed snapshot without rerunning the workflow (src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:3127-3137). That regresses aborted post-completion tasks: a user abort during completion-PR handling returns early with the task Aborted (src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1519-1542), while claim_task explicitly says aborted retries should start fresh (src/contexts/automation_runtime/task_service.rs:148-162). After this change, retrying that task will silently reuse the old completed run instead of starting a new one.

Treat a missing RunCompleted event as a hard reconciliation metadata error instead of fabricating Utc::now(). In try_reconcile_success, the matching RunCompleted timestamp is optional and falls back to wall-clock time when absent (src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1795-1818), even though the same function already fails closed for missing RunStarted metadata and the CLI reconciliation path treats a missing terminal event as corruption (src/cli/run.rs:317-327). Proceeding with a synthetic timestamp can misorder milestone history and hide a partially written/corrupt project journal behind seemingly successful reconciliation.

details.message: backend invocation failed for contract 'plan_and_implement' via 'claude': invalid Claude result JSON: expected value at line 1 column 1 (contract: plan_and_implement, result_len: 132, stdout_len: 1070, result_tail: "All three round-19 amendments are resolved. Quality checks pass (757 tests, clippy clean, fmt clean). The ExecutionPayload is above.", stderr_tail: "")

details.body: In `try_reconcile_success` (daemon_loop.rs ~line 1730-1740), when the RunStarted journal event is found but `run_id` is `None` (the `None => true` fallback branch when `run_id_from_task` is `None`), the code extracts `run_id` from `event.details.get("run_id")`. If that detail field is missing, the function returns a metadata error. However, the `run_id_from_task` fallback (`None => true`) selects the *latest* RunStarted event, which may not correspond to this task's actual run if another task manually triggered a run on the same project between the failure and the retry. The `persist_workflow_run_id` call before reconciliation mitigates this for new runs, but tasks dispatched before this code was deployed will lack `workflow_run_id` and hit the fallback. Consider adding a defensive log warning when the fallback path is used (i.e., `run_id_from_task` is `None`) so operators can identify tasks that may have picked up the wrong run_id from the journal.

src/contexts/automation_runtime/task_service.rs:152-162 and src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:681-703 / 913-918 only special-case `reconciliation_*` failures when the run snapshot is already `Completed`. A task that finished the run but failed later in `handle_completion_pr_with_cancellation` is marked `pr_runtime_failed`, so its retry still does remote-resume, rebase, and `ensure_project` even though no code will execute. Any unrelated git/auth/rebase failure can block the retry before it reaches PR recovery or success reconciliation, leaving a completed run stranded behind bookkeeping-only failure. Completed-run retries need a broader post-completion retry path, not a `reconciliation_*`-only check.

src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:867-873 persists `workflow_run_id` only after `dispatch_in_worktree` has already returned success, while src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1730-1746 falls back to the latest `RunStarted` event whenever that field is missing. A crash in that window leaves no durable task->run binding; if another run is started in the same project before retry, `try_reconcile_success` can reconcile the wrong run (or fail against the wrong `RunCompleted`), which violates the crash-safe idempotency requirement for this bead.

Quality checks pass (757 tests, clippy clean, fmt clean). The ExecutionPayload is above.", stderr_tail: "")

details.body: Add a test in success_reconciliation::tests that verifies a BvExitError (non-zero exit) whose stdout contains message-only JSON (e.g. `{"message":"No actionable items available"}`) produces HintCaptureOutcome::BvFailed, NOT NoRecommendation. This is the exact bug the BvError pattern match fix addresses (lines 289-292 of success_reconciliation.rs), but no test exercises the negative case. The existing test `capture_hint_failure_returns_bv_failed` uses BvNotFound, which doesn't cover the stdout-with-message scenario. The existing test `capture_hint_no_recommendation_on_message_only_response` only covers the BvParseError (exit 0) path.

In `persist_workflow_run_id` (daemon_loop.rs ~line 1636), when binding the run_id for a task that has `workflow_run_id: None` (either a legacy task or a crash-recovery case), the method picks the latest RunStarted event from the journal without any warning. If another run was started on the same project between the original dispatch and this retry, the method silently persists the wrong run_id. Downstream, `try_reconcile_success` trusts `workflow_run_id` from the task and skips the warning log (line 1741-1752 only fires when `run_id_from_task` is `None`). Add an eprintln warning in `persist_workflow_run_id` when it actively writes a run_id (as opposed to early-returning), mirroring the warning pattern in `try_reconcile_success`, so operators can correlate suspect bindings.

Fix post-completion retries so they still resume the preserved task branch when PR/branch state is needed. `src/contexts/automation_runtime/task_service.rs:152-173` turns every Completed-snapshot retry into `needs_remote_resume = false`, including `pr_runtime_failed`. But the previous worktree is disposed on failure (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:942-948`, `src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:3058-3077`), and `LeaseService::acquire` then recreates a fresh worktree from the default branch when resume is disabled (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/lease_service.rs:163-216`, `src/adapters/worktree.rs:510-521`). On retry, `handle_completion_pr` operates on that fresh checkout (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/pr_runtime.rs:102-117`), which can incorrectly see "no diff", close/skip the PR, or hit non-fast-forward push failures even though the completed branch existed before. Restrict the no-resume path to reconciliation-only retries, or otherwise resume the remote branch for PR-runtime/aborted completion retries.

In `persist_workflow_run_id` (daemon_loop.rs:1649), the `eprintln!` warning fires unconditionally on every successful binding — including the normal happy path for legacy tasks being retried for the first time. Consider gating this on a debug/trace level or adding a distinguishing flag so operators don't need to triage a warning that's expected behavior during the migration from pre-workflow_run_id tasks.

src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:701-708, 789-823, 881, 1450-1470, 1496-1510, 1535-1539, 1624-1664: `persist_workflow_run_id` eagerly stores the latest historical `RunStarted` before dispatch, then `mark_active` re-reads that value into `active_task`, and the post-dispatch call returns immediately on the `workflow_run_id.is_some()` guard. If the project journal already contains another run for the same project when the task is retried, `try_reconcile_success` will bind to that stale run id instead of the run that just completed.

src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:292-347 and src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1790-1821 only switch to `repair_task_run_with_disposition` when the existing terminal lineage row matches the same `started_at`, and the daemon derives `started_at` from collapsed lineage / `RunStarted` only. That omits the existing CLI repair logic for resumed runs and stale same-`run_id` terminal rows; `FsTaskRunLineageStore::finalize_task_run_internal` still rejects the non-repair path when `entry.started_at != started_at` (src/adapters/fs.rs:3832-3859). The CLI path already handles this via `same_named_terminal_attempt_exists` and `effective_attempt_started_at` (src/cli/run.rs:215-240, 347-369, 435-449).

details.message: backend invocation failed for contract 'plan_and_implement' via 'claude': schema validation failed for stage 'plan_and_implement': missing field `change_summary`

details.body: In `update_milestone_state` (success_reconciliation.rs:320), `exact_attempt_already_terminal` is computed as `same_named_terminal_attempt_exists || <narrower check>`, but the narrower check (adding `started_at` constraint) is strictly subsumed by `same_named_terminal_attempt_exists` (which already matches project_id + run_id + terminal). The `||` makes the second branch dead code. The variable name `exact_attempt_already_terminal` is misleading since it actually equals `same_named_terminal_attempt_exists`. Consider simplifying to just use `same_named_terminal_attempt_exists` or renaming for clarity.

On reconciliation-only retries (failure_class starts with 'reconciliation_'), the multi-repo daemon_loop success path still calls `handle_completion_pr_with_cancellation` before `try_reconcile_success`. For tasks where the original failure was reconciliation-only (PR was already created/merged successfully), re-running the PR handler is unnecessary work and could produce confusing log output or edge-case failures if the PR state has changed since the original run. Consider short-circuiting the PR handler for post-completion reconciliation retries, or at minimum adding a comment explaining why re-running it is safe.

Reconciliation-only retries are claimed without branch resume at src/contexts/automation_runtime/task_service.rs:152-182, but the daemon still reruns PR completion at src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:887-898 and src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1548-1561 after completed runs short-circuit at src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:3203-3213. That PR path pushes/checks the task branch and will close-or-skip the PR when the worktree is not ahead at src/contexts/automation_runtime/pr_runtime.rs:102-116. On a reconciliation_* retry, this can mutate or fail PR state even though the retry was supposed to do only local bead close/sync/milestone bookkeeping.

When workflow_run_id is missing, the new fallback binds the task to the latest RunStarted event in persist_workflow_run_id() at src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1644-1669 and again in try_reconcile_success() at src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1757-1787. The code already documents that this may be wrong if the project was rerun between failure and retry, but it still proceeds to close the bead, sync, and update milestone state against that guessed run. For older tasks or after a transient task-write failure, a manual rerun can therefore attach completion to the wrong run/timestamps instead of surfacing a reconciliation_metadata_error.

details.message: backend invocation failed for contract 'plan_and_implement' via 'claude': schema validation failed for stage 'plan_and_implement': missing field `change_summary`

details.body: In `try_reconcile_success` (daemon_loop.rs, around the run_id fallback logic near line 794), when `task.workflow_run_id` is None and `task.attempt_count > 1`, the function returns a metadata error. However, `attempt_count` is incremented by `mark_failed` + `retry_task` cycles, but the `persist_workflow_run_id` call happens BEFORE the outcome match. If `persist_workflow_run_id` succeeds, `workflow_run_id` will be Some and this guard never fires. But if `persist_workflow_run_id` silently fails (it only eprints on error and continues), AND the task is on attempt_count > 1, AND the task was dispatched before `workflow_run_id` was introduced, then we get a hard error that blocks reconciliation permanently with no self-healing path. Consider degrading this to a warning and proceeding with the journal fallback when the run has already completed (RunStatus::Completed confirms the journal's latest RunStarted is stable), since the risk of wrong-run binding is lower for completed runs than for in-progress ones.

Fix the retry safety guard in `src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1785-1807`. `retry_task` increments `attempt_count` from 0 to 1 on the first retry (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/task_service.rs:509-514`), but this code only fails closed when `attempt_count > 1`. As written, the first reconciliation retry still falls back to the latest `RunStarted` entry and can bind to a different manual rerun, which would close/sync the bead and rewrite milestone lineage for the wrong run.

Remove or harden the eager `workflow_run_id` persistence on post-completion retries in `src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:701-712`, `src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1490-1495`, and `src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1658-1689`. Both retry paths prefill `workflow_run_id` from the latest `RunStarted` before reconciliation, but that heuristic is exactly what the new `try_reconcile_success` guard is trying to avoid. If another run was started on the project after the original reconciliation failure but before the task retry, this code stores the newer run ID and the later reconciliation path treats it as authoritative.

In `try_reconcile_success` (daemon_loop.rs ~line 1889-1893), the `is_reconciliation_only_retry` variable at line ~889-893 uses `task.failure_class` which was read before `claim_task` (line 647). However, `claim_task` calls `clear_failure()` on the task. Since `task` is the pre-claim snapshot while `active_task` is the post-claim copy (with failure cleared), this is actually correct for reading the original failure class. BUT: the same `is_reconciliation_only_retry` variable is computed a second time at lines 889-893 inside the `match outcome` block, using the same `task` variable. Verify that `task` is still in scope and refers to the pre-claim snapshot at that point in the code. The pre-claim `task` binding at line 647 shadows the outer `task` parameter — this is correct. No actual bug, but the reliance on shadowing for correctness is subtle and worth noting in a comment.

In `persist_next_step_hint` (success_reconciliation.rs lines 424-460), the atomic write pattern (write to .tmp, then rename) is correct for POSIX, but on some Linux filesystems the rename is only durable after an `fsync` on the parent directory. Without fsync, a crash after rename could lose the file entry. Since hints are best-effort and non-blocking, this is acceptable — but worth documenting that the atomicity guarantee is against torn reads (truncated JSON), not against crash durability.

In `src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1770-1825`, `try_reconcile_success` still falls back to the latest `RunStarted` event whenever `workflow_run_id` is missing. Because `persist_workflow_run_id` is best-effort (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1675-1680`), a post-completion retry can legitimately reach this branch. If someone manually reran the same project between the original failure and the retry, this code will bind the daemon task to that newer run, then use its `RunCompleted` timestamp and run ID for reconciliation (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1912-1955`). That can close/sync the bead and rewrite milestone lineage for the wrong attempt instead of surfacing a needs-operator condition. The retry path needs a deterministic run binding or a fail-closed operator handoff here; guessing `latest RunStarted` is not safe.
